task_completion.description: Agent logged into the Splunk web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Web UI is visible in a web browser, indicating a successful login (typically showing the default home screen or Search & Reporting app).
A dashboard is visible with the exact title 'Data_Quality_Audit' in the Splunk UI.

task_completion.description: The dashboard contains at least three visualization panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least three distinct panels (charts, tables, single value displays, etc.) are visible on the 'Data_Quality_Audit' dashboard.

SPL queries visible in the search bar, search history, or panel edit screens show searches targeting indexes like 'security_logs', 'web_logs', 'system_logs', or 'index=*' (without querying '_internal').

task_completion.description: The '_indextime' field is incorporated into the dashboard queries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SPL queries visible in the search bar, search history, or panel edit screens explicitly include the '_indextime' field.

task_completion.description: The 'punct' field is incorporated into the dashboard queries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SPL queries visible in the search bar, search history, or panel edit screens explicitly include the 'punct' field.

task_completion.description: The 'len' eval function or 'linecount' field is incorporated into the dashboard queries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SPL queries visible in the search bar, search history, or panel edit screens explicitly include the 'len' function (e.g., eval length=len(_raw)) or the 'linecount' field.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Splunk Web UI rather than solely using terminal commands or API calls.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Splunk web interface in a browser being actively used to create the dashboard and run searches.

integrity.description: The dashboard was genuinely created within Splunk.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dashboard appears within the standard Splunk framing (navigation bar, app context, Splunk styling) and is not a mocked-up HTML page or text file.

integrity.description: The dashboard panels display actual queried data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visualizations (tables, charts) display populated data rows or graphs, indicating real log data was successfully queried and rendered, rather than empty panels or hardcoded text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dashboard must be named exactly 'Data_Quality_Audit' and contain a minimum of 3 visualization panels. The SPL queries powering these panels must incorporate the following specific fields/functions: '_indextime', 'punct', and either 'len' or 'linecount'. The queries must target non-internal indexes.
